description: Cullen Oaks resident reviews — management, surprise fees, slow maintenance, pests, and parking/break-in security complaints (substituted for Yelp, which is gated behind a DataDome CAPTCHA wall that blocks automated capture)
segments: Current Resident 716765 Resident • 2023 1.7 7/25/2023 The place is disaster, terribly infected by cockroaches. The roaches are every where. They are even in the microwave and fridge. Building has always a terrible smell and the rooms are very small and dingy

Current Resident 225680 Resident • 2021 - 2022 1.3 4/26/2022 NOT WORTH IT This place is extremely overvalued and the only staff you really ever see are Student RA's(which only a handful really care) and maybe some repair workers. Our parking lot gates have been open for the ENTIRE semester and multiple cars have had their catalytic converter stolen aswell as windows smashed in for things so small as airpods. The security cameras to my understanding "only work sometimes". The furniture in my building was really wore out and flaking everywhere. It has become a running joke that Cullen oaks is just plain bad around the area and is a last option resort. Also the rooms are pretty small and all my neighbors made a ridiculous amount of noise. The parking lot is always full due to the parking reinforcement kind of just giving up at one point and letting whoever they want park here which causes me to walk all the way from my parking zone. All complaints they receive it seems like they just make it seem the acknowledge it and after 2 weeks any slight fixes put in place vanish. Also the basketball court had the rims off during my entire lease although during my tour 2 weeks prior to… See More >

Former Resident 844984 Resident • 2017 - 2018 1.0 7/13/2018 The WORST place to live on campus. All the management cares about is money and they WILL manipulate you into getting it. While I was living there the manager on duty barged into my apartment at 10 o'clock AT NIGHT for a "surprise room check". They do not have a sense of privacy and are just looking to charge you some fee. No sense of moral. Racial discrimination among the RA's is also very real. I am a person of minority and have been subject to racism on many accounts. Completely unprofessional. The staff has NO manners or common sense, especially the managers. Other than management causing problems, this place wasn't the worst to live in. The area has gotten safer, but car break ins still occur frequently. The rooms are fine enough and the living room is pretty spacious if you get a phase 1 apartment. Other than that this place is a complete rip off.

Current Resident 424498 Resident • 2017 - 2018 1.7 5/12/2018 Some of the staff members were nice but many of them were 100% rude, stuck up, unprofessional, and unhelpful. The RA's smoke weed constantly and are extremely rude when asked for help. Make sure to read the lease agreement because they will overcharge you ridiculous fees for rules you didn't even know existed. Cars get broken into and no one does anything. Would be a horrible place to live mainly because of the management and employees.

uh student Resident • 2016 - 2018 1.0 4/19/2018 My experience has been absolutely terrible. The management sucks and they simply do not care. They refuse to talk to you and make you go through the student staff who do not know what to do. The washers and dryers NEVER work and i have had to go to friends places because the washers and dryers will be down weeks at a time. When i moved in, the room was not clean and it is not updated. The sofas have holes all in them, the beds are worn out and nosier because they are 20 year old cheap mattresses. The air conditioner leaked, it took them over two weeks to come out and look at it. The take pride in their free printing services, however, the printer never works and they never fix it. The elevators are down at least twice a week and the fire alarm goes off at 2am at least once every other week. The managers do not care and just want your money. They will "accidently" charge you with a late fee and then refuse to take it off?? They have security walking around but they don't enforce anything. The halls constantly smell like weed and… See More >

Allison_R302 Resident • 2017 - 2018 1.0 4/12/2018 Literally the only good thing about this place is location. They office staff do not care about your issues at all, they have made my time here so terrible I felt the need to write this review. I never write reviews because nothing has ever been as bad as this place. If you decide to live here make sure you never have any issues with anything ever because the office staff doesn't care. They will place you with a horrible roommate who complains about every little thing you do and they take their side and impose ridiculous, non-lease agreement, rules that you never sign or agree to. The smoke Alarms also go off all the time and because of the cheap wiring and construction there is nothing that can be done so you are stuck being woken up at 2 AM with a smoke alarm going off. Please, please, please do not sign a lease here. I made the mistake of doing so because of the location. It is not worth it.

Allison_M156 Resident • 2017 - 2018 3.0 3/1/2018 The student staff at Cullen Oaks is nice enough, and for the most part, they seem to do their job. Unfortunately, they seem to go through new management or managers every other month, which obviously can create inconsistency in service. I would not recommend living here if you are a light sleeper or like to study in quiet environments because you will hear a loud thump every time the person who lives above you takes a step. I feel that it's also important to point out that they will show you a really nice 2 bedroom 1 bathroom (Phase2) showroom, but the Phase 2 two bedroom one bath apartment you get is at least 25% smaller than the showroom. The rent for a tiny 2 bedroom apartment with a bathroom you have to share is $807 PER PERSON. Add that up. That means rent for a 502 square foot apartment is $1914. That doesn't seem right, does it? In addition to this outrageous price, utilities are also NOT fully covered! You have to pay whatever additional amount your electricity bill is over $20, which is very easy to do if you enjoy air conditioning. On the bill they give you,… See More >

Current Resident 295718 Resident • 2017 3.0 7/19/2017 I am a parent who has put 2 kids thru college, so I have dealt with various "apartments". This place is a cross between a dorm and an apartment. It is a decent place, has security, and maintains the property. The problem is with the management and the billing. The management is young, inexperienced and unprofessional. Most are college students. If there is a problem with the bill (which we have had), they will say that "the computer system was being updated". The will attempt to stick you with late charges, even if you try to pay online and are unable due to their error. If you can find a complex with better management, put you kids there.

Current Resident 835269 Resident • 2016 3.2 12/28/2016 DO NOT SIGN THE LEASE. GO ELSEWHERE
I know people get on here and are dramatic or tell outrageous stories to make things worse than what they actually were, but I can't stress this enough: don't live here.
-PRICING: The current running price for a 4 bedroom apartment is about $2,900 ($724 monthly per person) and there is access tall price increase coming soon. This is the cheapest room they offer. For that amount, you could get a few buddies together and rent a 2 story with a pool. All they care about is money. Always double check your monthly bill, there will be charges on there that they can't even explain half the time. They take advantage of the kids who can't or don't feel comfortable enough to live within an offcampus apartment deeper in the city, so they tax ludicrous prices. The deferment they offer is due before financial aid even has time to disperse. They're con artists
-STAFF: They don't run this place like a regular apartment. All of their policies are different so read the lease CAREFULLY. Always ask 3 times and get the responses in writing because each answer you get will be different, usually at your expense.… See More >

Current Resident 216042 Resident • 2016 1.0 11/22/2016 This property's amateur management never fails to surprise visitors! From bed bugs to fire alarm malfunctions at 5AM during exams, there's always something exciting going on at Cullen Oaks!
Here are a few tales from Cullen:
My friends who were supposed to move in together got assigned different rooms with other random people!
Rooms often need a deep cleaning upon arrival (only if you are someone who values cleanliness, of course)!
Rooms come with sofas...and the sofas come with holes!
We got pets! Bed bugs!
BYOMC: bring your own mattress cover! The beds are rock hard...and noisy, btw! My neighbor seems to have an exciting social life!
Talking about neighbors, they LOVE to party!!! There's always some noise going on around if you don't like to feel lonely - specially at night!
This week's bonus: ALL laundry washing machines are all broken in two floors of my building...this is the 4th day already! For the past 4 months they were unable to fix 2 of the 4 dryers, so I guess I'll have to handwash will Christmas.
There are better and cheapper places around! Save yourself the trouble, Cullen Oaks is not worth it.

Current Resident 561909 Resident • 2016 1.7 10/25/2016 Very bad place to say. Washers and dryers are in horrible condition, staff is very rude. Once I left my cougar card in my room and studied late night, so I called an on call RA at 1:45am and she didn't even let me in, I basically had to pound on the front door for the security guard to let me in. They rip you off for the amount your paying for. The only thing they got going for is how clean the rooms are when you first move in

Current Resident 817411 Resident • 2016 1.0 10/4/2016 This place is absolutely dreadful. The 'model' apartment that they show you is definitely not like the one that you will be living in, do not be fooled. They will scam you out of so much money and you will never have any idea what you are paying for. They cannot be any less concerned with the well-being of their residence and I have nothing good to say about the staff. Also, don't be surprised if the wifi gets cut off while you're taking an important exam because that will more than likely happen to you at least once. Also, if you like having clean clothes, good luck, because the washers are always broken. As soon as we moved in, the apartment was left dirty, the toilet was stained brown and yellow, the stove was left burned and crusted, and the ice maker never worked. They are always doing pointless work outside and I mean pointless, because I've never seen any improvements, by the way, they start bright and early. Do not live here unless it is your absolute last resort and you want to waste all of your money. If you are trying to choose between here and somewhere… See More >

Bellyn Resident • 2014 - 2016 1.0 6/21/2016 This place is a major rip off! Beware! You will lose your deposit. Even if you leave the place spotless they will charge you for repainting the walls and putting in new carpet. Make sure that you fill out a damage checklist because if you do not have one on file they will stick you for everything! We scrubbed the place and left it spotless but we're still charged for repainting the bedroom wall because of scuff marks, which can't be cleaned because the paint is cheap, flat and when you wipe off scuff marks the paint comes off. You will be billed for this.
Be prepared for your clothes to be stolen from the washing machines. This happened several times and management will do nothing. There are video cameras all around, but have not worked for years. Bike was stolen and when we asked for copy from the video camera that was right above the bike station, we were told by security that those cameras are broken.
Management is very unprofessional and they can not care less. They know there are so few places to live around there and all are equally as bad.
Do your research and every… See More >
Current Resident 299984 Resident • 2014 - 2016 5.0 3/24/2016 I loved living here. It was great

BigCheeseFatz Resident • 2015 - 2016 1.3 2/15/2016 This place is an absolute rip off. Not only do you have to pay rent for 2 months that you don't even live in the apartment, but they constantly charge you separate fees. $20 "convenience" fee every month if you pay online; which is easier and cheaper for them to process. Then if you need to do a deferment they give you deadlines that fall before you have received your financial aid money and charge you an additional $300 for not paying on time?!?!?! Also, the phone number they have listed never works and the staff on site is rude. Their goal is to bleed every once of money they can get from you. Go anywhere but here

Current Resident 326735 Resident • 2014 - 2016 1.5 1/9/2016 The office staff is the worst. They are the rudest individuals and make you feel unwelcomed when you walk into the office. Each of them always tells me a different answer to anything I have asked and they are so unorganized they are basically a joke. You would think that for the price of rent I pay for such a bad service, bad facilities, and bug/mold infested rooms wouldn't be happening but it is. I would never recommend anyone to even think about living here.
Current Resident 768421 Resident • 2015 1.3 9/12/2015 Absolutely horrible customer service. Charged me $400 at the end of the year for damage reported at the start of the lease.

Current Resident 862072 Resident • 2014 - 2015 2.3 9/5/2015 Cullen Oaks is a magical type of place where you can read all the informational brochures, go on a tour, memorize the contract and ordinances and still be shocked by things they do. SURPRISE, monthly room checks! SURPRISE, one "unauthorized gathering" can get you evicted! SURPRISE, they even use the emergency intercom to advertise for social events in Cullen (while the events are happening)! You'll be astounded at how little maturity they assume you have and how many times they threaten to evict residents for the smallest offenses.
"But I signed a contract!"
Doesn't matter they'll treat you like the unwanted freeloader you aren't (because you pay $600-800 a month). Did I mention utilities aren't included?
I received better treatment my two years in the freshman dorm at UH. The apartment vibe of Cullen Oaks is masked by naggy and negligent management (hard combination, but they pull it off well).
If you can get over careless employees, nosy RA's, a spectrum of new threats/rules every month, paper thin walls, roaches, more gnats than roaches, and nonexistent wifi then Welcome Home!
The common areas outside are pretty and they give you a flat screen TV with free cable.

Ddlewis1 Resident • 2014 - 2015 4.0 8/4/2015 These negative reviews about cullen oaks are very overdramatic! I will share my experience with you living in A building during 2014-2015. I had a 2 bed 2 bath fall and spring semester.
1. Cullen maybe is a little pricey but I had financial aid and it was cheaper than cambridge right next door and comes furnished, utilities included with electricity cap I never paid more than $3 extra for electricity.
2. There is security actually Theres a security post right infront of A building and main entrance where the security guards are there almost 24/7 and they walk around the parking lot. sometimes i even saw them walking through the apartment buildings.
3. I've seen 3 roaches (very small) in my floor 1 bathroom but not my 3rd floor apartment.
4. They made me move out of my 1st floor room after my rooommate graduated in december but they reimbursed $200 for making me move it was still a pain in the --- though.
Move in and out days are very short and you need to hurry and get your stuff out or they will charge you a late fee and take it out your deposit. You basically have 1 or… See More >

Current Resident 375742 Resident • 2012 - 2014 1.0 8/3/2015 PARENTS, IF YOU CARE FOR YOUR CHILDS SAFETY, DO NOT, I REPEAT, DO NOT MOVE THEM HERE. The worst apartment I have ever lived in. Spend less, and find a much nicer place off campus. If you live here, expect to be surrounded by a bunch of thugs and wanna be thugs. 80% of the tenants are ------ as ****. If you are an attractive girl, DO NOT walk around here by yourself at night. You will get harassed and quite possibly assaulted. Constant muggings in the parking lots and hallways. No security at all. The only thing that may be more absurd and ------ then the tenants and surroundings are of course, the management (dont listen to anything Chasity Taylor tells you, shes just trying to take your money). My 6 month old could run a better business. Dont move here unless you want to spend thousands of dollars to live in the ------, constantly get nagged by obnoxious thug tenants, and very possibly get mugged, murdered, or raped.

Current Resident 652758 Resident • 2013 - 2015 1.7 7/17/2015 There are literally a million reasons why I would not recommend this so called "apartment" complex to anybody! If I could give this place 0 stars I would. What I really dislike about Cullen Oaks is how horrible the management staff is. With the amount of money we pay to live there, they treat you as if you are living there for free.They are so unorganized, its not even funny. All they literally want is your money, they don't care about your health or your safety. I'm convinced at least 90% of the establishment is filled with mold. My first year living there I had mold in my windowpane, and all they did was paint over it. Like c'mon, if that alone is not enough reason, I don't know what is. When you move out, just expect unnecessary charges to your account. I was charged $200.00 for replacement of a tear in the carpet that was there when I moved in. Despite if you report damages when you move in, they will always find a reason to charge you for something. The biggest mistake I made in college was living in these disgrace of the term "apartments". Whoever may come… See More >

Current Resident 530895 Resident • 2015 1.2 5/21/2015 this apartment complex is a complete dump please do not sign a leasing agreement ...I have never experienced such poor management in all of my life ..my daughter lived in Cullen Oaks from January to May 2015 ...we just moved out of this hell hole ...The rent is ridiculous. ..you are literally paying more then your mortgage on a home to live in the projects...And calling them apartments
.Please!!!
The university as we will know is smack dab in the hood and you have a management staff of hood mentality students running the property ...completely disappointing..This property is poorly maintained... it hasn't been cleaned since it was built ..so you know what comes with FILTH. ..ROACHES ...This place is a DUMP...
Parents please don't waste your hard earned money.

Current Resident 168439 Resident • 2015 2.2 2/4/2015 I would give this establishment negative stars. SO unprofessional. The staff could care less. Use 'quick fixes' to solve extensive problems. Mildew and mold continuously found around the property, staff has complete disregard for the health and comfort of residents. There is absolutely no security. Staff takes their sweet time to fix any problem. Unclean, subpar housing. I would never recommend this location to anyone. Ever.

Current Resident 117657 Resident • 2014 2.0 10/13/2014 This is by far the worst apartment complex I have ever lived in. I have had nothing but problems with the staff, the apartments themselves are incredibly small and disgusting. I was moving here from Michigan and had to be down here for school to work at UH, so weeks ahead of time I was trying to arrange early move in. I would call every other day to see where they were in the process, and they kept telling me their manager was working on it. Finally, it got to the week I had to start driving down here, and I called to tell them I NEEDED an answer, and they told me their manager had went on vacation, even though all of the emails I sent got the automated response that this is their busiest time and they will get to us as soon as possible. I got to Houston, went straight to the office, and they told me they couldn't get me in until next week. So, I was pretty much stranded in Houston, even though they make you pay rent for July and August...
Literally the worst staff I've ever dealt with, my roomates floor has the shape… See More >

Current Resident 16934 Resident • 2009 - 2010 3.7 5/29/2014 I lived at Cullen Oaks during my freshman year in a four bedroom unit with a friend who I went to high school with. One of the other roommates was a good guy, the other, another good guy, graduated and moved out in December. All of us got along but kept to ourselves when the time called for. The graduates replacement? Some pot head who showed up one day in January, always at the apartment and smoked weed constantly. Yes it's college and I wasn't innocent by any means, but don't do it inside where everyone on the floor knows which unit it's coming from. Also, once this roommate moved in, food and dishes would begin magically disappearing. This was new as the three other roommates, as well as the graduate, would always share dishes. Oh well.
I never had a maintenance issue. Walls were paper thin to surrounding units. Our unit overlooked the parking lot where music would be blasting at all hours of the night. I always parked in the garage on either one of the top two floors. I never had a problem with safety/security, but was always smart. Monthly fire alarm "tests" for hours at a time… See More >
